metadata.name: fusd_core
metadata.description: fUSD — Solana-native overcollateralized CDP stablecoin engine (see ../../docs/fusion-docs.md)

instructions.name: accept_gov_authority
docs: Governance: STEP 2 — the proposed successor signs to ACCEPT the admin authority. Only now
does the live `gov_authority` move, so a typo'd / unheld proposal can never strand the
admin role (market onboarding, guardian rotation).

accounts.name: new_authority
docs: MUST equal `config.pending_gov_authority` — the proposed successor proves control by
signing. This is what makes the handoff two-step (the incoming key can't be a typo).

instructions.name: accept_inbound_authority
docs: Governance: STEP 2 — the proposed successor signs to ACCEPT the inbound authority. Only now
does the live `inbound_authority` move, so a typo'd / unheld proposal can never brick param
governance.

accounts.name: new_authority
docs: MUST equal `gov_gate.pending_inbound_authority` — the proposed successor proves control by
signing. This is what makes the handoff two-step (the incoming key can't be a typo).

instructions.name: adjust_rate
docs: Change the position's borrower-set rate, moving it to the matching redemption bucket.

instructions.name: borrow
docs: Mint fUSD against the position up to MCR and the market debt ceiling.

instructions.name: cancel_global_param_change
docs: Governance: CANCEL a queued global-param change before it executes.

instructions.name: cancel_param_change
docs: Governance: CANCEL a queued change before it executes. Gated on the inbound authority.

accounts.name: authority
docs: MUST equal `gov_gate.inbound_authority`. Reclaims the op's rent.

instructions.name: claim_coll_surplus
docs: Claim a position's liquidation collateral surplus (the bonus-collar remainder).

instructions.name: claim_reactor_gains
docs: Claim a depositor's realized seized-collateral gains.

instructions.name: close_position
docs: Close an empty CDP and reclaim its rent + liquidation bond.

instructions.name: deposit
docs: Deposit collateral into the caller's position.

accounts.name: owner
docs: `mut`: the under-bond top-up below debits lamports from the owner via a system transfer,
which requires the account writable even when the owner is not the tx fee payer.

instructions.name: execute_global_param_change
docs: Permissionless: EXECUTE a queued global-param change once its timelock has elapsed.

instructions.name: execute_param_change
docs: Permissionless: EXECUTE a queued change once its timelock has elapsed.

accounts.name: executor
docs: Anyone — execution is permissionless once the timelock elapses. Receives the op's rent.

instructions.name: fund_backstop
docs: Permissionless: top up the global backstop reserve with protocol-strengthening fUSD.

instructions.name: fund_buffer
docs: Permissionless: fund a market's insurance buffer with fUSD (realized fees / treasury / keeper).

instructions.name: guardian_derisk
docs: Guardian (independent of futarchy): pause NEW borrowing on a market for `pause_secs`
(clamped to `GUARDIAN_MAX_PAUSE_SECS`; auto-lifts; `0` lifts early). Monotonic de-risk only —
never touches existing positions, funds, repay, liquidation, or redemption.

accounts.name: guardian
docs: The de-risk guardian (must equal `ProtocolConfig.guardian`). Independent of gov_authority.

instructions.name: init_global_backstop
docs: Governance: one-time create the Global Backstop Reserve + its fUSD vault. Gated on
`config.gov_authority`. Ships inert (every param 0/off).

instructions.name: init_governance_gate
docs: Governance: create the bounded `GovernanceGate` (the param-tuning authority + the
fUSD-owned timelock). Gated on `config.gov_authority`.

instructions.name: init_insurance_buffer
docs: Governance: create a market's insurance buffer (the third loss-absorption tier).

instructions.name: init_market
docs: Governance: onboard a collateral as an isolated market + escrow vault.

accounts.name: collateral_mint
docs: The collateral asset. The onboarding gate is COMPLETE for the locked legacy-SPL-only
stance: `Account<token::Mint>` + `Program<Token>` reject any
Token-2022 mint at validation (AccountOwnedByWrongProgram) — legacy mints physically
cannot carry fee/hook/pausable/delegate/state extensions — and the handler rejects the
one hazard legacy CAN carry (a freeze authority). Pinned by the litesvm T22 regression.

instructions.name: init_market_oracle
docs: Governance: bind a market's oracle feeds + validation thresholds and create its
DEX-TWAP observation ring.

accounts.name: quote_mint
docs: The expected quote (USD-stable) leg of the sampled CLMM pool. Read once here to bind its
key + decimals into `MarketOracle` so `sample_twap` needs no Mint account at crank time.

accounts.name: market
docs: The market must already exist (init_market first).

instructions.name: init_protocol
docs: One-time: initialize `ProtocolConfig` and the fUSD mint.

accounts.name: program_data
docs: This program's `ProgramData` account (BPF upgradeable loader). Derived as the canonical
programdata PDA of THIS program, so it cannot be spoofed; `Account<ProgramData>` checks the
owner (= the upgradeable loader) and deserializes it. The constraint gates the one-time
bootstrap to the program's **upgrade authority** — only whoever can upgrade the program may
initialize it. This closes the initialize-front-run governance-capture vector: the global
`[b"config"]` PDA is deterministic, so without this gate anyone could land `init_protocol`
first and set `gov_authority`/`guardian` to themselves.

accounts.name: mint_authority
docs: possible via `invoke_signed` from inside borrow under the rules.

accounts.name: fusd_mint
docs: The fUSD mint. Created with mint authority = the PDA above and **NO freeze
authority** (omitted ⇒ `None`, irreversible) — the core censorship-resistance
guarantee. Legacy SPL Token program (not Token-2022).

instructions.name: init_reactor_pool
docs: Governance: create a market's Reactor Pool (vaults + the bounded S grid).

instructions.name: liquidate
docs: Permissionless: liquidate an under-MCR position via the Reactor Pool.

accounts.name: liquidator
docs: Permissionless caller; receives the SOL reserve bond + the collateral gas-comp.
Data-bearing accounts below are boxed: 13 inline `Account` payloads overflow the
4 KB BPF stack frame in `try_accounts` (measured 5504 B — same class as the
`InitReactorPool` fix).

accounts.name: liquidator_collateral_ata
docs: The liquidator's collateral ATA — receives the gas-comp skim (no-op when the market sets
`liq_gas_comp_bps == 0`, but always required). `token::authority = liquidator` pins it to the
caller, so it can't alias a program vault (which would make the skim a no-op self-transfer
and desync `total_collateral` from the vault balance).

accounts.name: insurance_buffer
docs: Tier 3: the per-market insurance buffer (protocol first-loss fUSD reserve). Required on every
liquidation so the waterfall always has its third tier; if empty it simply contributes 0 and
the residual falls through to the terminal `unhomed` → `shutdown`.

accounts.name: backstop
docs: Tier 3.5: the Global Backstop Reserve + its vault (OPTIONAL).
When BOTH are supplied, a liquidation whose loss spills past the local buffer may draw from the
reserve up to the per-market hybrid cap, before booking un-homed bad debt. When omitted (no
backstop, or a market that doesn't use it), `global_available == 0` and the waterfall is the
pre-backstop 4-tier. Boxed (liquidate is already stack-tight).

instructions.name: migrate_gov_authority
docs: Governance: STEP 1 of the two-step `gov_authority` (bootstrap/admin) handoff — the current
admin PROPOSES a successor. The live authority does not change until the successor calls
`accept_gov_authority`. Pass `Pubkey::default()` to cancel a pending handoff.

accounts.name: authority
docs: MUST equal the CURRENT `config.gov_authority` — the admin proposes its own successor.

instructions.name: migrate_inbound_authority
docs: Governance: STEP 1 of the two-step inbound-authority handoff — the current authority
PROPOSES a successor (e.g. launch multisig → MetaDAO Squads vault). The live authority does
not change until the successor calls `accept_inbound_authority`. Pass `Pubkey::default()`
to cancel a pending handoff. Gated on the CURRENT inbound authority.

accounts.name: authority
docs: MUST equal the CURRENT `gov_gate.inbound_authority` — governance proposes its own successor
(e.g. launch multisig → MetaDAO Squads vault).

instructions.name: open_position
docs: Open an (empty) CDP for the signer in a market (posts the SOL liquidation bond).

instructions.name: open_reactor_deposit
docs: Open an (empty) Reactor-Pool deposit for the signer.

instructions.name: provide_to_reactor
docs: Deposit fUSD into a market's Reactor Pool.

instructions.name: queue_global_param_change
docs: Governance: QUEUE a clamped GLOBAL backstop-param change behind the timelock.

instructions.name: queue_param_change
docs: Governance: QUEUE a clamped market-parameter change behind the timelock. Gated on the
gate's inbound authority (the MetaDAO → Squads → fUSD CPI target).

accounts.name: authority
docs: MUST equal `gov_gate.inbound_authority`. Pays the queued op's rent.

accounts.name: market
docs: The target market (real, program-owned `Market`); recorded into the op and re-checked at execute.

instructions.name: rebind_market_oracle_feeds
docs: Governance: rebind a market's oracle feed SOURCES (Pyth feed id / Switchboard feed
account / DEX-TWAP pools) for a feed migration.

accounts.name: authority
docs: MUST equal `config.gov_authority`.

instructions.name: redeem
docs: Permissionless: redeem fUSD for face-value collateral, draining the lowest non-empty rate
bucket first; candidate positions are passed as `remaining_accounts`.

instructions.name: refresh_market
docs: Permissionless: advance a market's interest accumulator.

accounts.name: cranker_fusd_ata
docs: OPTIONAL: an fUSD token account to receive the keeper reward — a cut (`Market.keeper_reward_bps`)
of the interest this crank mints; the cranker directs it to themselves. Constrained
to the fUSD mint only (the caller picks any fUSD account, typically their own ATA). When omitted,
or when `keeper_reward_bps == 0`, the WHOLE interest mints to the buffer (no reward). Permissionless:
whoever does the crank work earns the cut.

accounts.name: backstop
docs: OPTIONAL: the Global Backstop Reserve + its vault. When BOTH are supplied (and the reserve's
`cut_bps > 0` and it is below its cap), `backstop_cut_bps` of the post-keeper interest routes
here as second-loss capital; the rest stays in the LOCAL buffer (the majority). When omitted,
the whole post-keeper interest funds the local buffer (byte-identical to pre-backstop behavior).
The hot user paths never touch these — only this periodic crank.

instructions.name: repay
docs: Burn fUSD to repay the position's debt (capped at current debt).

instructions.name: sample_twap
docs: Permissionless: sample a configured Orca/Raydium CLMM pool's spot price into the market's
DEX-TWAP observation ring (the manipulation-resistance corridor).

accounts.name: cranker
docs: Permissionless caller. No authority check.

accounts.name: clmm_pool
docs: (must be a configured pool, owned by the matching venue program; discriminator / length /
sqrt-price bounds / mint-pair checked via `crate::clmm`). Never trusted by address alone.

instructions.name: set_guardian
docs: Governance: rotate/revoke the de-risk guardian (gated on `gov_authority`; immediate, so a
compromised guardian key can be revoked fast). `Pubkey::default()` disables the guardian.

accounts.name: authority
docs: Must equal `config.gov_authority` (the bootstrap/admin authority).

instructions.name: set_oracle_program_ids
docs: Governance: update the bounded-updatable oracle PROGRAM IDs (Pyth receiver /
Switchboard on-demand) so the Pyth core migration (~2026-07-31) needs no redeploy.

accounts.name: authority
docs: MUST equal `config.gov_authority`.

instructions.name: settle_bad_debt
docs: Governance: burn fUSD to retire realized bad debt (recap settlement).

accounts.name: authority
docs: MUST equal `gov_gate.inbound_authority`. The fUSD is burned from this signer's ATA.

instructions.name: shutdown
docs: Permissionless: terminally wind a market down when it is failing (TCR < SCR on a fresh price,
or sustained oracle failure). Closes borrow + ordered redeem; opens `urgent_redeem`.

accounts.name: cranker
docs: Permissionless caller (signs only to carry the tx). No authority check.

instructions.name: sweep_protocol_collateral
docs: Governance: recover retained protocol-owned (un-homed) collateral (recap sweep).

accounts.name: authority
docs: MUST equal `gov_gate.inbound_authority`.

accounts.name: recipient
docs: Where the recovered collateral is sent (governance recapitalization account). Must not
alias the market vault: a governance fat-finger passing the vault itself would make the
transfer a no-op self-transfer while the counter is debited — silent value-stranding.

instructions.name: update_price
docs: Permissionless: aggregate Pyth + Switchboard + the DEX TWAP into the market's cached
collateral price (`spot`) and mint-freeze mode. The borrow blocker until run.

accounts.name: cranker
docs: Permissionless caller (signs only to carry the tx / pay fees). No authority check.

accounts.name: config
docs: Global config (read-only) — carries the bounded-updatable oracle program IDs the parsers
verify the feed accounts' owners against (so a Pyth core migration doesn't force a redeploy).

accounts.name: pyth_price_update
docs: `PYTH_RECEIVER_PROGRAM_ID`, anchor discriminator, `VerificationLevel::Full`, and bound to
`market_oracle.pyth_feed_id`. Never trusted by address.

accounts.name: switchboard_feed
docs: (owner == `SWITCHBOARD_ON_DEMAND_PROGRAM_ID`, key == `market_oracle.switchboard_feed`).
Absent (or a degraded value) ⇒ `aggregate` freezes mints but still prices off Pyth.

instructions.name: urgent_redeem
docs: Permissionless: redeem fUSD for face-value collateral from ANY position in a shut-down market
(unordered, 0-fee, last price). The wind-down floor; candidates via `remaining_accounts`.

instructions.name: withdraw
docs: Withdraw collateral, keeping the position at/above MCR if it has debt.

instructions.name: withdraw_backstop_excess
docs: Governance: withdraw ABOVE-CAP excess from the reserve to a recipient. Gated on the gate's
inbound authority; never below `reserve_cap`.

accounts.name: authority
docs: MUST equal `gov_gate.inbound_authority` (the governance fund-movers gating pattern).

accounts.name: recipient_fusd_ata
docs: The recipient fUSD account — must NOT be the reserve vault itself (a self-transfer would debit
the counter while stranding the value).

instructions.name: withdraw_from_reactor
docs: Withdraw fUSD from a Reactor Pool (capped at the compounded deposit).

instructions.name: withdraw_surplus
docs: Governance: withdraw accrued redemption-fee surplus collateral.

accounts.name: authority
docs: MUST equal `gov_gate.inbound_authority`.

accounts.name: recipient
docs: Where the surplus collateral is sent (governance-chosen treasury). Must not alias the
market vault: a governance fat-finger passing the vault itself would make the transfer a
no-op self-transfer while the counter is debited — silent value-stranding.

errors.name: Unauthorized
errors.msg: Unauthorized: signer is not the configured governance authority

errors.name: ParamOutOfBounds
errors.msg: Parameter is outside the compile-time clamp range

errors.name: CollateralHasFreezeAuthority
errors.msg: Collateral mint has a freeze authority and cannot be onboarded

errors.name: OracleUnavailable
errors.msg: Oracle price is stale, unset, or feeds disagree (mint frozen)

errors.name: StalePrice
errors.msg: Cached collateral price is too old to act on

errors.name: BelowMinCollateralRatio
errors.msg: Operation would leave the position below its minimum collateral ratio

errors.name: DebtCeilingExceeded
errors.msg: Market debt ceiling exceeded

errors.name: ZeroAmount
errors.msg: Amount must be non-zero

errors.name: InsufficientCollateral
errors.msg: Insufficient collateral in the position

errors.name: PositionHealthy
errors.msg: Position is at or above its minimum collateral ratio — cannot liquidate

errors.name: ReactorPoolTooSmall
errors.msg: Reactor Pool deposits cannot fully absorb this liquidation (tier-2 redistribution not yet wired)

errors.name: InsufficientReactorDeposit
errors.msg: Insufficient Reactor Pool deposit balance

errors.name: ReactorGridExhausted
errors.msg: Reactor Pool grid exhausted (scale/epoch out of range) — migration required

errors.name: MathOverflow
errors.msg: Arithmetic overflow

errors.name: NoRedistributionRecipients
errors.msg: Liquidation cannot complete: the Reactor Pool is empty and no other position can absorb the redistribution

errors.name: RedistributionAccumulatorOverflow
errors.msg: Redistribution accumulator overflow — migration required

errors.name: PositionNotEmpty
errors.msg: Position still holds debt or collateral and cannot be closed

errors.name: NothingToRedeem
errors.msg: Nothing to redeem: no non-empty rate bucket or no valid candidate

errors.name: WrongRedemptionBucket
errors.msg: Redemption target is not in the lowest non-empty rate bucket

errors.name: DuplicateRedemptionTarget
errors.msg: Duplicate redemption target in the candidate list

errors.name: TimelockNotElapsed
errors.msg: Governance timelock has not elapsed — the queued change cannot execute yet

errors.name: TimelockMarketMismatch
errors.msg: The market account does not match the queued change's target market

errors.name: MintFrozen
errors.msg: New mints are frozen: the oracle aggregate is degraded (stale/wide-conf/divergent)

errors.name: InvalidPriceUpdate
errors.msg: Pyth price update is invalid: wrong owner, unverified, wrong feed id, or non-positive price

errors.name: InvalidSwitchboardFeed
errors.msg: Switchboard feed account is invalid: wrong owner, wrong key, or unparseable

errors.name: InvalidClmmPool
errors.msg: CLMM pool account is invalid: wrong owner/program, discriminator, length, sqrt-price bounds, or mint pair

errors.name: TwapSampleRejected
errors.msg: TWAP sample rejected: timestamp not strictly newer than the last, or closer than the minimum inter-sample interval (anti-flood)

errors.name: GuardianPaused
errors.msg: New borrowing is paused by the guardian de-risk brake (auto-lifts; does not affect repay/liquidation/redemption)

errors.name: MarketShutdown
errors.msg: Market is shut down (terminal): borrow and ordered redemption are closed; use urgent_redeem

errors.name: MarketNotShutdown
errors.msg: Market is not shut down: urgent_redeem is only valid after shutdown

errors.name: ShutdownConditionNotMet
errors.msg: Shutdown condition not met: the market is neither below SCR nor in sustained oracle failure

errors.name: RateLimitExceeded
errors.msg: Net fUSD issuance would exceed the market's rate-limit cap for the current window

errors.name: CcrRestricted
errors.msg: Operation restricted: it would leave the market below its critical collateral ratio (CCR band)

errors.name: LiquidationGracePeriod
errors.msg: Liquidations are paused by the on-resume grace window after an oracle staleness halt — borrowers have a window to cure

errors.name: InterestRateOutOfBounds
errors.msg: Borrower interest rate is outside the allowed range [MIN_USER_RATE_BPS, MAX_USER_RATE_BPS]

errors.name: NoCollateralSurplus
errors.msg: No liquidation collateral surplus to claim

errors.name: NoPendingAuthority
errors.msg: No pending inbound authority to accept (propose one first via migrate_inbound_authority)

errors.name: DebtBelowMinimum
errors.msg: Operation would leave the position below the market's minimum debt: repay fully or stay at/above min_debt

errors.name: TooManyRedemptionCandidates
errors.msg: Too many redemption candidate accounts (exceeds MAX_REDEMPTION_CANDIDATES)

errors.name: InsufficientProtocolCollateral
errors.msg: Requested amount exceeds the available protocol-owned collateral (surplus or un-homed remainder)

errors.name: CollarExceedsMcr
errors.msg: Liquidation bonus collar is not fundable at the MCR boundary (requires 100% + liq_bonus <= MCR, or bonus 0)

errors.name: ParamCombinationInvalid
errors.msg: Parameter values are individually in range but jointly invalid for this market's config

errors.name: VaultReconciliationFailed
errors.msg: Collateral vault holds less than the tracked ledger sum — accounting drift detected, reverting

errors.name: InvalidRecipient
errors.msg: Recipient must not be a protocol vault (a self-transfer would debit the counter while stranding the value)

errors.name: InsufficientBackstopExcess
errors.msg: Requested amount exceeds the global backstop reserve's above-cap excess (the cap is the protective floor)

errors.name: OracleDivergent
errors.msg: Liquidations are paused: a fresh primary feed grossly disagrees with a present secondary (oracle divergence) — redemptions and repay stay open

types.name: BackstopDrawn
docs: A liquidation drew on the global backstop (tier 3.5) to absorb debt the local buffer couldn't.

types.name: BackstopFunded
docs: fUSD flowed INTO the reserve — a permissionless top-up (the per-market interest cut emits its own
per-market signal; this is the explicit donation path). `total_contributed` is cumulative.

types.name: BackstopInitialized
docs: The Global Backstop Reserve was created.

types.name: BackstopWithdrawn
docs: Governance withdrew above-cap excess from the reserve. `total_withdrawn` is cumulative.

types.name: BadDebtEvent
docs: Un-homed bad debt was booked (the tier-4 terminal loss) — the alert PoR monitoring pages on.
Emitted alongside [`LiquidationEvent`] when `unhomed > 0`.

types.name: BadDebtSettled
docs: Governance burned fUSD to retire realized bad debt (`settle_bad_debt`; the recap settlement).

types.name: BufferFunded
docs: An external deposit into the insurance buffer (`fund_buffer`).

types.name: CollSurplusClaimed
docs: A borrower claimed the collateral surplus a collared liquidation returned (`claim_coll_surplus`).

types.name: DexTwap
docs: Per-market self-maintained DEX TWAP observation ring (zero-copy). Appended by the
permissionless `sample_twap` crank; read by `update_price` as the manipulation-resistance
corridor (NEVER a primary price). PDA `[b"twap", collateral_mint]`. See fusion-docs.md;
ring math + tests in `fusd_oracle::twap`.
Fields MIRROR `ObservationRing<TWAP_RING_CAPACITY>` field-for-field (both `repr(C)`):
embedding the const-generic type directly would force an anchor-lang `IdlBuild` dep
into the pure fusd-oracle crate, so instead `ring()`/`ring_mut()` reinterpret this
account as the ring via a Pod cast — sound iff the layouts are identical, which the
`const` size assert plus the behavior round-trip test below enforce. Do not reorder
or resize fields without updating `ObservationRing` (and vice versa).

types.name: EpochToScaleToSum
docs: The bounded epoch×scale collateral-gain-per-unit grid — one `u128` per cell (single
collateral per isolated market). Direct indexing `epoch * REACTOR_MAX_SCALES + scale`; the
`offset` math reverts if exhausted (never wraps → no silent gain loss). Zero-copy, following
Hubble/USDH's `[u128; N]` precedent. PDA `[b"ess", collateral_mint]`.

types.name: GlobalBackstopReserve
docs: The system-wide **Global Backstop Reserve** — bounded shared SECOND-LOSS capital (one per protocol).
PDA `[b"backstop"]`.
A protocol-owned **fUSD reserve** funded by a minority cut of every market's realized interest
(`refresh_market`), drawn automatically in the liquidation waterfall as tier 3.5 — AFTER a market's
own local insurance buffer (first-loss) is exhausted, BEFORE un-homed bad debt — up to a per-market
hybrid draw cap. It mutualizes only the narrow tail where a contained local failure would otherwise
surface as a system-wide backing shortfall (confidence contagion), WITHOUT coupling normal market
operations: it never moves user collateral between pools, and draws are rule-based (never a
discretionary governance allocation).
Its fUSD balance is protocol-owned and excluded from any position/market backing or health
computation — consumed only via the balanced loss-absorption flow in `liquidate`. Param tuning runs
through the TIMELOCKED `GlobalParam` flow; every param ships 0/off.

fields.name: fusd_vault
docs: The fUSD reserve vault (authority = this PDA). Its token balance IS the reserve balance.

fields.name: cut_bps
docs: Cut of post-keeper realized interest routed here from each market (bps). 0 = unfunded.

fields.name: reserve_cap
docs: Reserve-level cap (v1 ABSOLUTE fUSD). Above it, the funding cut reverts to the local buffer.

fields.name: draw_base_allowance
docs: Per-market draw base allowance (fUSD) — access independent of contribution.

fields.name: draw_k_bps
docs: Per-market contribution multiplier (bps): + `draw_k_bps/10_000 · Market.global_contributed`.

fields.name: draw_ceiling_share_bps
docs: A single draw may take at most this fraction (bps) of the LIVE reserve balance.

fields.name: draw_debt_share_bps
docs: Cumulative draws for a market may not exceed this fraction (bps) of its own debt.

fields.name: total_contributed
docs: Σ inflows: interest cuts (across all markets) + gov top-ups. `vault == total_contributed −
total_absorbed − total_withdrawn` (the reserve-solvency invariant).

fields.name: total_absorbed
docs: Σ debt the reserve has absorbed (burned its fUSD against) across tier-3.5 draws.

fields.name: total_withdrawn
docs: Σ above-cap excess withdrawn by governance.

fields.name: _reserved
docs: Forward-compat reserve (e.g. the debt-relative-cap snapshot pointer fast-follow). Carve new
fields from the HEAD; old accounts' zeroed bytes decode as the `0 = off` sentinel.

types.name: GlobalParam
docs: Which bounded GLOBAL (Global Backstop Reserve) parameter a governance change targets. Mirrors
[`MarketParam`] but for the system-wide backstop (no per-market target). **APPEND-ONLY** for the
same persisted-borsh-tag reason — see [`MarketParam`]; pinned by `global_param_tags_are_pinned`.

types.name: GovAuthorityMigrated
docs: Step 2 — the successor accepted; the live admin authority moved.

types.name: GovAuthorityProposed
docs: Step 1 of the two-step `ProtocolConfig.gov_authority` handoff.
`pending == Pubkey::default()` means a pending handoff was canceled.

types.name: GovernanceGate
docs: The bounded governance gate. PDA `[b"gov_gate"]`. The sole authorizer of timelocked param
changes (`queue_param_change`). `inbound_authority` is MIGRATABLE — repoint from a guarded-
launch multisig to the MetaDAO DAO's Squads vault PDA later (an earlier PoC proved that path).
fusion-docs.md.

fields.name: inbound_authority
docs: The authority allowed to QUEUE param changes — a launch multisig at first, the MetaDAO
Squads vault PDA in production. Migratable via the TWO-STEP handshake
(`migrate_inbound_authority` proposes → `accept_inbound_authority` the new key signs).

fields.name: pending_inbound_authority
docs: The proposed next inbound authority, pending its own acceptance. `Pubkey::default()` ⇒ no
handoff in flight. The live `inbound_authority` only changes when the holder of THIS key
signs `accept_inbound_authority`, so a typo'd / unheld proposal can never brick governance
(it simply can never be accepted, and the current authority can re-propose).

fields.name: timelock_secs
docs: The fUSD-owned timelock delay (seconds) between queue and execute. Bounded by
`[MIN_GOV_TIMELOCK_SECS, MAX_GOV_TIMELOCK_SECS]`; Squads itself runs `time_lock = 0`,
which is exactly why fUSD supplies its own.

fields.name: queue_nonce
docs: Monotonic counter assigning each queued op its own `TimelockedParam` PDA.

types.name: GuardianDerisked
docs: The guardian (re)set a borrow pause (`guardian_derisk`); `paused_until <= now` is an early lift.

types.name: GuardianRotated
docs: Governance rotated/revoked the guardian (`set_guardian`).

types.name: InboundAuthorityMigrated
docs: Step 2 — the successor accepted; the live authority moved.

types.name: InboundAuthorityProposed
docs: Step 1 of the two-step inbound-authority handoff. `pending == Pubkey::default()`
means a pending handoff was canceled.

types.name: InitMarketArgs
docs: Inputs to `init_market`. (Real compile-time clamps for these land with the params
milestone; here we enforce only basic sanity.)

fields.name: mcr_bps
docs: Minimum collateral ratio, bps. Must be >= 100%.

fields.name: debt_ceiling
docs: Debt ceiling, fUSD-native units.

fields.name: reserve_lamports
docs: Per-position SOL liquidation bond (lamports). Bounded by `MAX_RESERVE_LAMPORTS`; 0 disables.

fields.name: liq_gas_comp_bps
docs: Liquidator collateral gas-comp (bps). Bounded by `MAX_LIQ_GAS_COMP_BPS`; 0 disables.

fields.name: liq_bonus_bps
docs: Liquidation bonus collar (bps). Bounded by `MAX_LIQ_BONUS_BPS`; **0 = collar OFF** (seize-all).
Deploy scripts pass `DEFAULT_LIQ_BONUS_BPS`. Governance-tunable later via `MarketParam::LiqBonus`.

fields.name: bucket_width_bps
docs: Redemption rate-bucket width (bps). Bounded by `MIN/MAX_BUCKET_WIDTH_BPS`.

fields.name: redemption_fee_bps
docs: Flat redemption fee (bps). Bounded by `MAX_REDEMPTION_FEE_BPS`; 0 disables.

types.name: InitMarketOracleArgs
docs: Inputs to `init_market_oracle`. Thresholds are clamped at the compile-time bounds in
`constants.rs` (defaults there are placeholders pending the backtesting pass).

fields.name: pyth_feed_id
docs: Pyth price-feed id (NOT an account address).

fields.name: switchboard_feed
docs: Switchboard on-demand feed account.

fields.name: orca_pool
docs: Orca Whirlpool to sample (`Pubkey::default()` = none). At least one pool required.
Validated structurally (owner / discriminator / mint pair) on EVERY `sample_twap`,
not here — a misconfigured pool yields no samples, never a bad price.

fields.name: raydium_pool
docs: Raydium CLMM pool to sample (`Pubkey::default()` = none).

fields.name: price_band_lower_ray
docs: Plausibility band, RAY-scaled USD per native collateral unit. Each bound
`0` = disabled; when both are set they must be `>= MIN_PRICE_BAND_RATIO` apart (a coarse rail,
not a tight opinion). Default `(0, 0)` = off — byte-identical to the pre-band oracle behavior.

fields.name: liq_max_divergence_bps
docs: Liquidation-divergence threshold (bps). `0` = disabled; otherwise clamped
`<= MAX_LIQ_DIVERGENCE_BPS` and intended LOOSER than the mint deviation thresholds.

types.name: InitProtocolArgs
docs: Inputs to `init_protocol`.

fields.name: gov_authority
docs: Migratable inbound governance authority (e.g. the MetaDAO Squads vault PDA).

fields.name: guardian
docs: Guardian — de-risk only, independent of futarchy/Squads.

types.name: InsuranceBuffer
docs: Per-market **insurance buffer** — protocol first-loss capital, the third liquidation
loss-absorption tier (RP → redistribution → **buffer** → un-homed; fusion-docs.md). It is a
pure **fUSD reserve**: on a liquidation the RP and other positions can't fully absorb, the buffer
**burns its own fUSD** to extinguish the remaining debt (the matching seized collateral stays in the
market vault as protocol-owned backing). Funded from realized fees only (no treasury seed);
it may run empty, in which case the waterfall falls straight to the terminal `unhomed` → `shutdown`.
The buffer's fUSD balance is **protocol-owned and excluded from any position/market backing or
health computation** — it is consumed only via the balanced loss-absorption flow in `liquidate`
(stock-reconciliation discipline). One per market; authority of its own fUSD vault.

fields.name: collateral_mint
docs: The market (collateral mint) this buffer backs.

fields.name: fusd_vault
docs: The fUSD reserve vault (authority = this buffer PDA). Its token balance IS the buffer balance.

fields.name: total_funded
docs: Cumulative fUSD funded into the buffer (observability / proof-of-reserves).

fields.name: total_absorbed
docs: Cumulative debt (fUSD) the buffer has absorbed (burned) across liquidations (observability).

types.name: InterestMinted
docs: `refresh_market` minted accrued interest: the buffer/keeper split for PoR + keeper
monitoring. Emitted only when something was minted.

fields.name: to_backstop
docs: The cut routed to the Global Backstop Reserve (global second-loss capital). 0 when the backstop
accounts aren't supplied / cut disabled / reserve at cap.

fields.name: unminted_remaining
docs: Backlog left unminted (non-zero only in the absurd >u64 case).

types.name: LiquidationEvent
docs: A liquidation, with the full five-tier waterfall breakdown (`reactor_offset + redistributed +
buffer_absorbed + backstop_absorbed + unhomed == debt` — the absorb conservation).

fields.name: debt
docs: Realized present-value debt extinguished/redistributed (fUSD-native).

fields.name: seized_collateral
docs: Collateral seized (native), inclusive of the gas-comp skim and the RP/redist shares.

fields.name: gas_comp
docs: Liquidator's collateral gas-comp skim (native).

fields.name: coll_surplus
docs: Collateral the bonus collar returned to the borrower as a claimable surplus (native).

fields.name: backstop_absorbed
docs: Absorbed by the global backstop reserve (tier 3.5).

fields.name: spot
docs: The cached oracle price the health check + seizure priced against.

types.name: Market
docs: Per-collateral isolated market. PDA `[b"market", collateral_mint]`.
A hot account in the per-collateral write lane (fusion-docs.md). Migrates to
`#[account(zero_copy)]` when the redemption rate-bucket bitmap and Reactor
Pool land. Debt accounting is the Liquity-v2 / BOLD **weighted-debt-sum** model:
each position carries its own `user_rate_bps`, and the market
accrues interest in O(1) off the two aggregates below.

fields.name: collateral_vault
docs: Program-owned escrow token account holding this market's collateral.

fields.name: agg_recorded_debt
docs: Σ recorded (present-value) debt across the market's positions, in fUSD-native units. Kept exact
at every touch (the aggregate interest folds into it in `accrual::accrue`; per-position
realizations only move debt between positions, never the total). BOLD `aggRecordedDebt`.

fields.name: agg_weighted_debt_sum
docs: Σ `recorded_debt_i · user_rate_bps_i` (bps scale) — the weighted-debt sum that makes interest
accrual O(1): pending interest over `dt` = `agg_weighted_debt_sum · dt / (SECONDS_PER_YEAR ·
10_000)`. Maintained by an add-then-subtract delta on every position touch. BOLD
`aggWeightedDebtSum`; bps scale keeps it in `u128` with vast headroom.

fields.name: unminted_interest
docs: Interest folded into `agg_recorded_debt` but not yet **minted** into existence. Accumulates on
every accrual touch; `refresh_market` mints it as fUSD into the insurance buffer and zeroes it
(the lazy mint seam — keeps the buffer-vault + mint off the hot path). The supply invariant is
`circulating fUSD == agg_recorded_debt − unminted_interest + bad_debt`.

fields.name: last_update_ts
docs: Unix timestamp of the last aggregate-interest accrual (BOLD `lastAggUpdateTime`).

fields.name: spot
docs: Cached collateral price: RAY-scaled fUSD-native per 1 native collateral unit
(OSM-style cache). 0 until set. Populated by the oracle crank; until then the
dev-only `dev_set_price` (feature `dev-oracle`) sets it for tests, so production
builds simply cannot borrow until the real oracle is wired.

fields.name: debt_spot
docs: Cached HIGH (debt) collateral price: RAY-scaled fUSD-native per 1 native collateral unit,
= `price + k·σ` (the asymmetric `debt_price`). **Liquidation eligibility and the
seize conversion price off this, never `spot`** (which a trunk refactor had collapsed onto):
under price uncertainty a position is liquidated only when it is underwater at the OPTIMISTIC
valuation, so a wide confidence band can't drive a destructive, irreversible liquidation on
noise. `borrow`/`withdraw` (LTV), redemption payout, and the CCR/SCR gauges keep using the
conservative LOW `spot` — pessimism is protective for *extending* or *winding down* risk,
optimism is protective for *destroying* a position. Written together with `spot` by
[`Market::commit_fresh_spot`] (shares its freshness clock). 0 until first priced; a market
priced before this field existed reads 0 and is fail-closed un-liquidatable until re-cranked
(the `0 = unset` sentinel). Carved from `_reserved`.

fields.name: mcr_bps
docs: Minimum collateral ratio, bps (e.g. 12_000 = 120%).

fields.name: debt_ceiling
docs: Debt ceiling in fUSD-native units.

fields.name: l_coll
docs: Cumulative redistributed-collateral per unit staked (1e18-scaled). Liquity `L_ETH`.

fields.name: l_art
docs: Cumulative redistributed **recorded (present-value) debt** per unit staked (1e18-scaled). Liquity
v2 `L_boldDebt`. (Under the weighted-debt model the redistributed quantity is recorded debt, not
normalized art; the `fusd_math::redistribution` math is unit-agnostic, so only the unit changed.)
Redistributed debt is **parked non-interest-bearing** here until a recipient's next touch folds it
into `recorded_debt` and re-weights it at the recipient's OWN rate (BOLD).

fields.name: last_coll_redist_error
docs: Floor-division residuals carried between redistributions, so repeated liquidations don't drift.

fields.name: total_stakes
docs: Σ of all positions' stakes — the redistribution denominator.

fields.name: total_collateral
docs: Tracks the collateral vault's token balance **exactly** (the invariant the tests check).
It is `>= Σ position.ink`: floor dust from redistribution stays here as protocol-favoring
over-collateralization until a position realizes its share.

fields.name: total_stakes_snapshot
docs: System snapshots captured after each liquidation, feeding the `compute_stake` ratio that
keeps `Σ stake == total_stakes` exact across redistributions.

fields.name: reserve_lamports
docs: Per-position SOL bond posted at open, paid to the liquidator on liquidation, refunded on
close. Bounded by `MAX_RESERVE_LAMPORTS`; 0 disables. Fixed per position at open-time.

fields.name: liq_gas_comp_bps
docs: Liquidator collateral gas-comp (bps of seized collateral), skimmed before the
RP/redistribution split. Bounded by `MAX_LIQ_GAS_COMP_BPS`; 0 disables.

fields.name: liq_bonus_bps
docs: Liquidation **bonus collar** (bps): a liquidation seizes collateral worth at most
`debt · (1 + liq_bonus_bps/10000)`; the surplus above that is returned to the borrower as
`Position.coll_surplus`. Governance-adjustable (`MarketParam::LiqBonus`) within
`[0, MAX_LIQ_BONUS_BPS]`; **0 = collar OFF** (seize the whole position).

fields.name: total_coll_surplus
docs: Σ of all positions' `coll_surplus` (native collateral) — the liquidation surpluses held in the
collateral vault but owed to borrowers (not backing any position). Makes the vault invariant
`vault == total_collateral + surplus_collateral + total_coll_surplus + protocol_collateral`
checkable from `Market` alone (proof-of-reserves). Bumped on a collared liquidation; drained by
`claim_coll_surplus`.

fields.name: bucket_width_bps
docs: Rate-bucket width (bps) — quantizes `user_rate` into the bitmap's 256 buckets. Bounded by
`MIN/MAX_BUCKET_WIDTH_BPS`.

fields.name: redemption_fee_bps
docs: Flat redemption fee (bps), governance-adjustable within `MAX_REDEMPTION_FEE_BPS`; 0 disables.

fields.name: surplus_collateral
docs: Collateral retained from redemption fees, held in the collateral vault but not backing any
position. Part of the 4-term vault invariant `vault == total_collateral + surplus_collateral
+ total_coll_surplus + protocol_collateral` (see `reconcile.rs`). Governance-withdrawable via
`withdraw_surplus`. Surplus buffer.

fields.name: mint_frozen
docs: Oracle-driven mint gate. Written by `update_price` from the `fusd_oracle`
aggregate mode: `true` when the aggregate is degraded (stale / wide-conf / cross-oracle or
TWAP divergence). `borrow` reverts when set; repay, liquidation, and redemption ignore it
(they keep using `spot`, which stays a conservative price even when frozen). Carved from
`_reserved` — no SPACE change.

fields.name: guardian_paused_until
docs: Guardian de-risk: new borrowing is paused while `now < guardian_paused_until` (unix secs).
Set ONLY by `guardian_derisk` (gated on `ProtocolConfig.guardian`), clamped to at most
`now + GUARDIAN_MAX_PAUSE_SECS` so it auto-lifts and can never ratchet the market shut. A
pure de-risk lever — it blocks only `borrow`; repay, withdraw, liquidation, and redemption
ignore it (never touches user funds or the peg floor). fusion-docs §7.2.

fields.name: shutdown
docs: Terminal per-market wind-down flag (fusion-docs §4.x). Set ONCE by the
permissionless `shutdown` when the market is failing (TCR < `scr_bps`, or sustained oracle
failure); **irreversible**. Closes `borrow` and ordered `redeem`; opens `urgent_redeem`
(unordered, 0-fee, face value). Carved from `_reserved`.

fields.name: scr_bps
docs: Shutdown collateral ratio (bps): the aggregate threshold below which `shutdown` may fire
(the market-level analog of a position's `mcr_bps`). Set at `init_market` to `DEFAULT_SCR_BPS`.

fields.name: rl_cap
docs: Max net fUSD issuance per `RATELIMIT_WINDOW_SECS` (fUSD-native). Governable (`MarketParam`);
**0 = disabled** (no limit). `borrow` consumes, `repay` restores; the bucket refills over the
window. Liquidation/redemption/urgent_redeem are exempt.

fields.name: rl_accrued
docs: Current net-outflow pressure in the bucket (fUSD-native), decayed by elapsed time on each touch.

fields.name: rl_last_update
docs: Unix timestamp of the last rate-limiter update (for the time-decay).

fields.name: ccr_bps
docs: CCR borrow-restriction band threshold (bps; fusion-docs.md). Governable (`MarketParam`);
**0 = disabled**. When `> 0` and the market's aggregate TCR is below it, `borrow` and
`withdraw` revert (`CcrRestricted`) — risk-increasing ops only; liquidation/redemption and
de-risking ops stay open, and the band fails open on a stale price. Never expands liquidation.

fields.name: liq_grace_until
docs: On-resume liquidation grace deadline (slot). Armed by [`Market::commit_fresh_spot`] when a
fresh price recovers from a staleness halt (prior gap > `MAX_PRICE_STALENESS_SLOTS`): set to
`now + LIQ_RESUME_GRACE_SLOTS`. `liquidate` additionally requires `slot >= liq_grace_until`, so
borrowers who couldn't act during the outage get a window to cure before a stale-then-fresh
price can cascade. 0 = no active grace (default); never re-cleared — a later halt just re-arms a
later deadline. Liquidation-only: redemption / `urgent_redeem` are oracle-independent and are
NEVER gated by it. fusion-docs.md.

fields.name: liq_divergence_until
docs: On-divergence liquidation pause deadline (slot). Armed by `update_price` when a FRESH
primary grossly disagrees with a PRESENT secondary (`fusd_oracle` `liq_divergent`): set to
`now + LIQ_DIVERGENCE_GRACE_SLOTS`, re-armed (monotone `max`) on every divergent crank, so when
convergence returns the pause self-clears one grace later (a snap-back can't instantly cascade).
`liquidate` additionally requires `slot >= liq_divergence_until` — a manipulated/briefly-bad
primary the secondaries visibly disagree with cannot drive liquidations. Liquidation-ONLY:
redemption, `urgent_redeem`, and repay are NEVER gated by it (the peg floor must
always clear). 0 = no active pause (default; the gate is off until governance sets a non-zero
`MarketOracle.liq_max_divergence_bps`). Carved from `_reserved`.

fields.name: bad_debt
docs: Realized **un-homed bad debt** (present-value fUSD): debt a liquidation could NOT extinguish
because RP + redistribution + the insurance buffer were all insufficient (the waterfall's
`unhomed`; fusion-docs.md). This fUSD remains in circulation with no backing debt — the
loss the buffer/recapitalization must eventually cover. Accumulates as terminal liquidations
occur; surfaced for proof-of-reserves. A non-zero value coincides with `shutdown` (the wind-down).
NOTE: the un-homed position's collateral is retained PROTOCOL-OWNED in the market vault (it backs
the wind-down) and tracked in `protocol_collateral` — so treat `bad_debt` as GROSS
circulating-unbacked fUSD; the offsetting collateral is recovered via `sweep_protocol_collateral`
(deployed against this loss off-chain), not double-counted as freely available.

fields.name: shutdown_reason
docs: Why the market was shut down (`SHUTDOWN_REASON_*`; 0 = not shut down). The named terminal-recovery
reason — set alongside `shutdown` by `shutdown` (SCR / oracle failure) or `liquidate` (un-homed bad debt).

fields.name: min_debt
docs: Minimum position debt (fUSD-native) — the dust floor (Liquity `MIN_NET_DEBT`). A
borrow/repay must leave the position at `recorded_debt == 0` or `>= min_debt`. Governable
(`MarketParam::MinDebt`) within `[0, MAX_MIN_DEBT]`; **0 = disabled**. Non-retroactive (gates only
new ops, never force-closes). Prices out the lowest-bucket dust-stuffing redemption grief.

fields.name: rate_adjust_cooldown_secs
docs: Premature rate-adjustment cooldown (secs) — the BOLD anti-gaming knob. When `> 0`, an
`adjust_rate` within this window of the position's last change/open is charged an upfront fee of
`cooldown`-seconds of interest at the new rate. Governable (`MarketParam::RateAdjustCooldown`)
within `[0, MAX_RATE_ADJUST_COOLDOWN_SECS]`; **0 = disabled**. Stops reactive rate-jumping to dodge
the redemption queue.

fields.name: keeper_reward_bps
docs: Keeper reward (bps) — the cut of the interest `refresh_market` mints that is paid to the cranker
(rest to the buffer), the self-funding keeper incentive. Governable
(`MarketParam::KeeperReward`) within `[0, MAX_KEEPER_REWARD_BPS]`; **0 = disabled**. Self-funding
(a split of already-minted interest, never a fresh mint) and spam-proof. Carved from `_reserved`.

fields.name: protocol_collateral
docs: Retained PROTOCOL-OWNED collateral (native) — the un-homed liquidation remainder (`coll_r`) that
had no redistribution recipient, so it sits in the collateral vault backing NO position.
Tracked separately from `total_collateral` (which only ever backs live positions + redistribution
dust) so the vault invariant stays exact and the amount is recoverable in O(1): `bad_debt`'s
offsetting collateral. Bumped on a tier-3/4 liquidation; drained by `sweep_protocol_collateral`
(governance, deploys it against `bad_debt`).

fields.name: global_contributed
docs: Cumulative fUSD this market has CONTRIBUTED to the Global Backstop Reserve (the interest cut).
A *local* write (this market's own lane — parallelism-safe),
it feeds the contribution-weighted arm of the per-market draw cap. Observability + fairness.

fields.name: global_drawn
docs: Cumulative fUSD this market has DRAWN from the Global Backstop Reserve (tier-3.5 absorbs). Enforces
the per-market draw cap across repeated draws (a market in slow decline can't re-draw past its bound).

fields.name: _reserved
docs: Forward-compat reserve (RP pointers, oracle refinements, ...). WIDENED 4 → 64 bytes
pre-launch: the carve-from-`_reserved` additive-upgrade path (used for
`mint_frozen`, `shutdown`, `keeper_reward_bps`) must survive the upgradeable Phases 1–3 —
post-launch a Borsh account cannot grow without realloc, so headroom is free now and
impossible later. Carve new fields from the HEAD of this reserve; old accounts' zeroed
bytes must decode as the new field's documented `0 = disabled/none` sentinel.

types.name: MarketInitialized
docs: A new isolated market (`init_market`).

types.name: MarketOracle
docs: Per-market oracle configuration: feed bindings + validation thresholds (fusion-docs.md).
PDA `[b"oracle", collateral_mint]`. Read-only on the hot path; written at init and
(later) by governance setters within the compile-time clamps in `constants.rs`.
The thresholds mirror `fusd_oracle::OracleConfig` — `update_price` converts and calls
`aggregate`. Stored as the smallest sufficient ints (bps fit u16; the clamps guarantee it).

fields.name: pyth_feed_id
docs: Pyth price-feed id (32 bytes, NOT an account address — bound via
`get_price_no_older_than(.., feed_id)`).

fields.name: switchboard_feed
docs: Switchboard on-demand feed this market accepts quotes for.

fields.name: orca_pool
docs: Orca Whirlpool pool sampled by `sample_twap`. `Pubkey::default()` = not configured.

fields.name: raydium_pool
docs: Raydium CLMM pool sampled by `sample_twap`. `Pubkey::default()` = not configured.

fields.name: collateral_decimals
docs: The collateral mint's decimals (copied from the mint at init; mirrors `Market.collateral_decimals`).

fields.name: quote_mint
docs: The expected quote (USD-stable) mint of the sampled pool — the OTHER leg of the collateral/USD
pair. `sample_twap` requires the pool's two mints == {collateral_mint, quote_mint}.

fields.name: quote_decimals
docs: The quote mint's decimals (bound to the real mint at init). Used to whole-token-adjust the
CLMM `sqrt_price` for Whirlpool (which stores no decimals); cross-checked against Raydium's
in-account decimals.

fields.name: max_conf_bps
docs: Freeze mints if Pyth conf/price exceeds this (bps).

fields.name: max_deviation_bps
docs: Pyth ↔ Switchboard agreement band (bps).

fields.name: twap_max_divergence_bps
docs: DEX-TWAP divergence corridor (bps).

fields.name: max_age_secs
docs: Feed staleness cutoff (seconds).

fields.name: k_bps
docs: Asymmetry: collateral `price − k·σ`, debt `price + k·σ` (bps of σ).

fields.name: liq_max_divergence_bps
docs: Liquidation-divergence threshold (bps), mirroring
`fusd_oracle::OracleConfig::liq_max_divergence_bps`. `0` = disabled. When set, a fresh primary
disagreeing with a present secondary by more than this arms `Market.liq_divergence_until`,
pausing LIQUIDATIONS only (never redemptions/repay). Set LOOSER than the mint
deviation thresholds. INIT-ONLY in v1 (clamped `[0, MAX_LIQ_DIVERGENCE_BPS]`). Carved from
`_reserved`.

fields.name: _reserved
docs: Forward-compat reserve. WIDENED 32 → 64 bytes pre-launch (layout-freeze checklist): a Borsh
account cannot grow without realloc post-launch, so carve-from-`_reserved` headroom is free
now and impossible later. Carve new fields from the HEAD; old accounts' zeroed bytes must
decode as the new field's `0 = disabled/none` sentinel. Holds feed-rebind and future
oracle refinements (e.g. a second Pyth feed for LST redemption-rate pricing).

types.name: MarketParam
docs: Which bounded market parameter a governance change targets — the live, clamped set of
post-init tunables, and the payload a timelocked op carries.
Deliberately excludes the non-retroactivity-sensitive params (the per-position SOL reserve
bond and the redemption `bucket_width`): bonds are fixed at open and `Position.bucket` is
stored, so those must not be retroactively re-applied to existing positions — they get
dedicated, explicitly non-retroactive handling with the registry work.
**APPEND-ONLY.** The borsh tag is the variant's declaration ordinal, and it
is PERSISTED — inside queued `TimelockedParam` accounts and in every emitted
`ParamChangeQueued/Executed` event. Removing or reordering a variant would silently RE-KEY
every in-flight queued op (a queued `LiqBonus` change executing as `Ccr`) and corrupt
historical event decoding. Rules: new variants are appended LAST; a variant is deprecated by
leaving it in place as a tombstone whose `validate_param` arm returns
`FusdError::ParamOutOfBounds` (so stored ops carrying it can never execute); a tag byte is
never reused. The `market_param_tags_are_pinned` test below breaks CI on any violation.

types.name: McrRaiseGraceArmed
docs: An executed governance MCR RAISE armed the market-wide liquidation grace window
(`liq_grace_until`, monotone max). A silently-armed liquidation pause would
be invisible to monitors — this is the alert/forensics hook for the raise-cycling trade-off.

fields.name: user_rate_bps
docs: Borrower-chosen **annual interest rate** (bps) — the real accrual rate AND the redemption
rate-bucket key. Validated to `[MIN_USER_RATE_BPS, MAX_USER_RATE_BPS]`.

types.name: OracleFeedsRebound
docs: Governance rebound a market's oracle feed SOURCES (feed id / SB account / DEX pools).

types.name: OracleProgramIdsUpdated
docs: Governance updated the bounded-updatable oracle PROGRAM IDs (e.g. the Pyth core
migration). A monitorable, rare admin event: the feed-account owner checks now verify against
the new IDs.

fields.name: prev_value
docs: The value the param held immediately before this change applied (the
forensic Prv/New trail; indexers and incident response reconstruct any param's history
from the event stream alone, without replaying program logic).

types.name: Position
docs: A user's CDP (trove). PDA `[b"position", collateral_mint, owner]`.
Only the owner's transactions write it, so all distinct users' borrow/repay/adjust
operations run in parallel under Sealevel (fusion-docs.md).

fields.name: ink
docs: Locked collateral, native units.

fields.name: recorded_debt
docs: Recorded (present-value) debt in fUSD-native units, as of `last_debt_update` (BOLD
`Troves[id].debt`). Interest accrued since then is folded in on the next touch
(`accrual::realize`); this is the actual debt — no `art*rate` normalization.

fields.name: user_rate_bps
docs: Borrower-chosen **annual interest rate** (bps), the real accrual rate (Liquity v2 user-set rates).
Validated to `[MIN_USER_RATE_BPS, MAX_USER_RATE_BPS]` at borrow/`adjust_rate`. Also the redemption
rate-bucket key — interest accrual never changes it, so redemption ordering is stable.

fields.name: last_debt_update
docs: Unix timestamp of this position's last debt realization (BOLD `lastDebtUpdateTime`); the start of
the interval over which `accrual::realize` charges `recorded_debt · user_rate_bps · dt` of interest.

fields.name: stake
docs: Collateral stake = `ink * total_stakes_snapshot / total_collateral_snapshot` (Liquity
`_computeNewStake`). Weights this position's share of redistributed debt + collateral.

fields.name: redist_l_coll_snapshot
docs: Snapshots of the market's `l_coll`/`l_art` at this position's last touch (Liquity
`rewardSnapshots`); pending gains accrue from here.

fields.name: reserve_lamports
docs: SOL liquidation bond actually posted (lamports), held on this account on top of rent. Fixed
at open from the market's then-current `reserve_lamports`, so a later governance change can't
retroactively alter it. Paid to the liquidator on liquidation (then 0); refunded on close.

fields.name: bucket
docs: Redemption rate-bucket this position is currently counted in — valid iff `recorded_debt > 0`
(the position joins a bucket when it first takes on debt and leaves when debt hits 0). Stored
explicitly (not re-derived) so a later `bucket_width_bps` change can't mis-target the
decrement of the bucket it actually joined.

fields.name: coll_surplus
docs: Collateral (native units) returned to this owner by the liquidation **bonus collar** — the
surplus above `debt · (1 + liq_bonus_bps)` that a liquidation did NOT seize (fusion-docs.md).
Held in the market collateral vault (NOT in `ink`, NOT staked — so a liquidated owner can't
inherit redistributed debt on the leftover) and withdrawn via `claim_coll_surplus`. The market
vault invariant is `vault == total_collateral + surplus_collateral + total_coll_surplus +
protocol_collateral`, where `Market.total_coll_surplus == Σ Position.coll_surplus` aggregates
this field.

fields.name: last_rate_adjust_ts
docs: Unix timestamp of this position's last interest-rate change (set at `open_position`, updated on
each `adjust_rate`). Drives the premature-rate-change cooldown/fee (BOLD anti-gaming):
an `adjust_rate` within `Market.rate_adjust_cooldown_secs` of this time is charged an upfront fee.

fields.name: _reserved
docs: Forward-compat reserve. WIDENED 6 → 32 bytes pre-launch: keeps the
carve-from-`_reserved` additive-upgrade path alive through the upgradeable Phases 1–3
(post-launch a Borsh account cannot grow without realloc). Carve from the HEAD; zeroed
bytes on old accounts must decode as the new field's `0 = disabled/none` sentinel.

types.name: PositionUpdated
docs: A position-touching user op (open/deposit/withdraw/borrow/repay/adjust_rate/close — `op` is the
`POSITION_OP_*` tag). `amount` is the op-specific quantity (collateral or fUSD moved; the new
rate for adjust_rate; 0 for open/close); the remaining fields are the position's POST-op state,
so a liquidation keeper can track health without fetching the account.

types.name: PriceCommitted
docs: An `update_price` crank ran: the post-aggregate oracle state (the oracle heartbeat — staleness
monitors alarm when these stop). `fresh`/`spot` reflect the committed cache; `mint_frozen` the
aggregate mode; `plausible` whether the aggregate passed the C6 band (a `fresh && !plausible`
run withholds the commit — a monitorable "implausible price observed" alert).

types.name: ProtocolCollateralSwept
docs: Governance swept retained protocol-owned (un-homed) collateral (`sweep_protocol_collateral`).

fields.name: bad_debt
docs: The realized loss this recovery is being deployed against (unchanged by the sweep itself).

types.name: ProtocolConfig
docs: Global, read-mostly config. PDA `[b"config"]`.
Passed read-only on the hot path so unlimited concurrent ops share it without
serializing (fusion-docs.md). Written almost never.

fields.name: gov_authority
docs: Migratable inbound governance authority — the MetaDAO DAO's Squads vault PDA
(`[b"multisig", multisig, b"vault", 0]`). The `GovernanceGate` is the sole
writer of bounded params; this is the authority it checks. fusion-docs.md.

fields.name: guardian
docs: Guardian: de-risk-only and INDEPENDENT of futarchy/Squads, so a frozen DAO
cannot freeze fUSD's emergency response. fusion-docs.md.

fields.name: deployer
docs: Deployer that ran `init_protocol` (informational).

fields.name: fusd_mint
docs: The fUSD mint (legacy SPL Token, freeze authority = None, mint authority = PDA).

fields.name: bump
docs: Canonical bump.

fields.name: pending_gov_authority
docs: Pending successor for `gov_authority` (two-step propose/accept handoff, mirroring
`GovernanceGate.pending_inbound_authority`). `Pubkey::default()` = no handoff in
flight. The live `gov_authority` moves ONLY when the pending key itself signs
`accept_gov_authority`, so a typo'd / unheld proposal can never brick the admin role
(it just can't be accepted; the current authority re-proposes). Carved from the head
of `_reserved` (offsets of all prior fields unchanged; SPACE unchanged).

fields.name: pyth_receiver_program_id
docs: Pyth receiver program that must own every `PriceUpdateV2` account `update_price` parses.
Bounded-updatable by `gov_authority` via `set_oracle_program_ids` so the Pyth
**core program migration (~2026-07-31)** — which changes the receiver program ID — can be
absorbed WITHOUT redeploying an immutable program. Seeded at `init_protocol` from the
compile-time `PYTH_RECEIVER_PROGRAM_ID` (the genesis default). NEVER `Pubkey::default()`
(a zero program ID would brick the crank). Carved from `_reserved`.

fields.name: pyth_receiver_program_id_alt
docs: A SECOND accepted Pyth receiver program (Pyth core upgrade). `update_price` accepts
a `PriceUpdateV2` owned by EITHER `pyth_receiver_program_id` OR this — the on-chain analog of
Pyth's "dual-fetch" guidance, giving a ZERO-DOWNTIME cutover across the dual-running window
(and making it irrelevant whether we launch before, during, or after the 2026-07-31 migration).
Seeded at `init_protocol` to `PYTH_RECEIVER_PROGRAM_ID_UPGRADED` so the cutover needs no gov
action at all. `Pubkey::default()` = disabled (only the primary is accepted). After the old
contract is fully deprecated, gov promotes (primary = upgraded) and clears this for
defense-in-depth. Carved from `_reserved`.

fields.name: switchboard_program_id
docs: Switchboard On-Demand program that must own every `PullFeedAccountData` account.
Same bounded-updatable rationale as `pyth_receiver_program_id`; seeded from the compile-time
`SWITCHBOARD_ON_DEMAND_PROGRAM_ID`. (Switchboard is NOT part of the Pyth core migration.)
Carved from `_reserved`.

fields.name: _reserved
docs: Reserved for fields added as flows land (registry pointer, DEX program IDs, ...).
fusion-docs.md.
NOTE: there is deliberately NO global emergency flag. The only emergency levers are
the per-market, rule-based ones (guardian pause-new-debt, permissionless `shutdown`).
A dead `emergency: bool` lived here until 2026-06-12; it was removed so that
"no global kill switch" is grep-verifiable — a dormant flag is exactly
the surface a future setter would colonize.

types.name: ProtocolInitialized
docs: One-time protocol genesis (`init_protocol`).

types.name: ReactorDeposit
docs: A depositor's stake in a Reactor Pool. PDA `[b"reactor_dep", collateral_mint, owner]`.
Realize-on-interaction: provide/withdraw fold the accrued collateral gain into
`pending_collateral_gain` and reset the snapshot.

fields.name: deposited_fusd
docs: Recorded deposit at last interaction (Liquity's `initialDeposit`).

fields.name: pending_collateral_gain
docs: Realized-but-unclaimed seized collateral (native units).

types.name: ReactorDepositUpdated
docs: A Reactor-Pool depositor op (`op` is the `REACTOR_OP_*` tag). `fusd_amount` is the fUSD moved
(provide/withdraw; 0 for claim); `collateral_paid` is the seized-collateral gain paid out
(claim; 0 otherwise); `deposited_fusd` is the POST-op compounded deposit.

types.name: ReactorPool
docs: Per-market Reactor Pool: fUSD depositors who absorb liquidated debt and earn the seized
collateral at a discount, in O(1) per liquidation. PDA `[b"reactor", collateral_mint]`.
Scalar fields mirror `fusd_math::reactor_pool::PoolState`. fusion-docs.md.

fields.name: fusd_vault
docs: fUSD deposit vault (authority = this RP PDA).

fields.name: coll_vault
docs: Seized-collateral vault awaiting depositor claims (authority = this RP PDA).

fields.name: epoch_to_scale_to_sum
docs: The bounded epoch→scale→sum grid account (zero-copy).

fields.name: p
docs: Running product `P` (1e18-scaled); starts at `DECIMAL_PRECISION`.

fields.name: total_deposits
docs: Current total fUSD deposited.

types.name: RebindOracleFeedsArgs
docs: Inputs to `rebind_market_oracle_feeds`. The full feed-binding set is re-supplied (the caller
passes the current value for any binding it is not changing) and re-validated exactly as
`init_market_oracle` validates them at birth. Thresholds, the plausibility band, the asymmetry
factor, and the TWAP guards are NOT touched — only the feed sources move.

types.name: RedemptionBitmap
docs: Per-market redemption-targeting bitmap (zero-copy). `words` marks non-empty rate buckets — bit
`k` is set iff bucket `k` holds ≥1 position with debt — and `redeem` finds the lowest non-empty
bucket over it via find-first-set. `counts[k]` is bucket `k`'s member count; the bit flips only
on a bucket's empty↔non-empty transition. PDA `[b"redeem_bitmap", collateral_mint]`.
fusion-docs.md; math in `fusd_math::rate_bucket`.

fields.name: zombie_count
docs: Member count of the redemption **zombie pen**: positions that carry debt but are no
longer normal redemption targets — collateral-exhausted (`ink == 0`, unredeemable) or sub-
`min_debt` dust. Parked OUTSIDE `words`/`counts` so they can never wedge or clog the normal
find-first-set ordering; a pen member carries `Position.bucket = ZOMBIE_BUCKET` and rejoins a
real bucket when a touch restores its health. `u64` (not `u32`) keeps the zero-copy struct
free of tail padding (`Pod`-clean: words 32 + counts 1024 + 8 stays 8-aligned).

types.name: RedemptionEvent
docs: An ordered (bitmap-targeted) redemption (`redeem`): totals across the batch.

fields.name: collateral_paid
docs: Collateral paid to the redeemer (native, net of fee).

fields.name: fee_collateral
docs: Fee collateral retained in `Market.surplus_collateral` (native).

fields.name: bucket
docs: The lowest non-empty bucket the batch drained.

fields.name: candidates
docs: Candidates submitted (validated, before in-bucket skips).

types.name: ShutdownEvent
docs: The market's terminal wind-down tripped (`shutdown`, or a tier-4 liquidation). Emitted exactly
once per market — on the false→true transition. `reason` is the `SHUTDOWN_REASON_*` constant.

types.name: SurplusWithdrawn
docs: Governance withdrew accrued redemption-fee surplus collateral (`withdraw_surplus`).

types.name: TimelockedGlobalParam
docs: A single queued, timelocked GLOBAL-param change. PDA `[b"gtimelock", nonce_le]` (distinct prefix
from the per-market `TimelockedParam`). Shares the `GovernanceGate` (inbound authority + timelock +
nonce); applied to the `GlobalBackstopReserve` by `execute_global_param` once `now >= eta`.

types.name: TimelockedParam
docs: A single queued, timelocked parameter change. PDA `[b"timelock", nonce_le]`. Created by
`queue_param_change`; applied + closed by `execute_param_change` once `now >= eta` (anyone
may execute), or closed unexecuted by `cancel_param_change`. fusion-docs.md.

fields.name: nonce
docs: The op's nonce (the `GovernanceGate.queue_nonce` value at queue time; also the PDA seed).

fields.name: eta
docs: Earliest unix timestamp at which `execute_param_change` may apply this op.

fields.name: market
docs: The market this op targets (re-checked at execute).

fields.name: param
docs: The parameter and its new value (re-clamped at execute).

types.name: TwapSampled
docs: A `sample_twap` crank appended a DEX observation (the TWAP-liveness heartbeat).

types.name: UrgentRedemptionEvent
docs: A shutdown wind-down redemption (`urgent_redeem`): unordered, 0-fee, last price.
